components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Adaptive laboratory evolution was performed via a series of passages in 50 mL glucose minimal medium (50 mL of M9 minimal medium with 20 mmol/L glucose)

components.name: ammonium chloride (NH4Cl)
components.source: standard:M9 minimal medium (Cold Spring Harbor / Sigma M9 minimal salts formulation)
components.evidence: The M9 minimal medium used for strains aerobic growth was supplemented with 10 mL/L of Wolfe's minerals and 4.5 mg/L of vitamin B1. [paper names M9 minimal medium; NH4Cl is the standard M9 nitrogen source]

components.name: disodium phosphate (Na2HPO4)
components.source: standard:M9 minimal medium (Cold Spring Harbor / Sigma M9 minimal salts formulation)
components.evidence: M9 minimal medium [paper names M9; Na2HPO4 is a standard M9 phosphate/buffer salt]

components.name: monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.source: standard:M9 minimal medium (Cold Spring Harbor / Sigma M9 minimal salts formulation)
components.evidence: M9 minimal medium [paper names M9; KH2PO4 is a standard M9 phosphate/buffer salt supplying potassium]

components.name: sodium chloride (NaCl)
components.source: standard:M9 minimal medium (Cold Spring Harbor / Sigma M9 minimal salts formulation)
components.evidence: M9 minimal medium [paper names M9; NaCl is a standard M9 bulk salt]

components.name: magnesium sulfate (MgSO4)

components.evidence: M9 minimal medium [paper names M9; MgSO4 (~2 mM) is the standard M9 Mg and sulfate source]

components.source: standard:M9 minimal medium (Cold Spring Harbor / Sigma M9 minimal salts formulation)
components.evidence: M9 minimal medium [paper names M9; CaCl2 (~0.1 mM) is the standard M9 calcium source]

components.name: thiamine hydrochloride (vitamin B1)
components.evidence: The M9 minimal medium used for strains aerobic growth was supplemented with 10 mL/L of Wolfe's minerals and 4.5 mg/L of vitamin B1.

components.name: EDTA
components.concentration: 5 mg/L (0.5 g/L in MD-TMS stock, added 10 mL/L)
components.source: stock:Wolfe's minerals (ATCC MD-TMS, Cat. No. MD-TMS)
components.evidence: Wolfe's minerals (Cat. No. MD-TMS)... supplemented with 10 mL/L of Wolfe's minerals; ATCC MD-TMS composition: EDTA 0.5 g/L

components.evidence: ATCC MD-TMS composition: MgSO4.7H2O 3.0 g/L (added at 10 mL/L)

components.evidence: ATCC MD-TMS composition: FeSO4.7H2O 0.1 g/L (added at 10 mL/L)

components.name: molecular oxygen (O2)
components.evidence: Air was sparged into the liquid at a rate of 12 mL/min to provide oxygen [aerobic growth throughout]

media.base_medium: M9 minimal medium (standard) + Wolfe's minerals (ATCC MD-TMS) + vitamin B1, aerobic, glucose 20 mmol/L

sources_chased.what: paper Methods (Chemicals and reagents / ALE sections)
Wolfe's minerals stock composition (ATCC Cat. No. MD-TMS)
standard M9 salt concentrations (Na2HPO4, KH2PO4, NaCl, NH4Cl, MgSO4, CaCl2)
sources_chased.how: standard M9 formulation (paper names 'M9 minimal medium' but does not spell out salt amounts)

media.notes: Medium = standard M9 minimal salts + 10 mL/L Wolfe's minerals (ATCC MD-TMS, 1:100 dilution) + 4.5 mg/L thiamine (B1); carbon = 20 mmol/L glucose (10 mmol/L in 13C-tracer runs); aerobic. The trace-metal block (Mo, Se, W, Ni, B, Al, EDTA) was hidden inside the MD-TMS stock and was absent before; it is expanded here from the ATCC catalog formulation, so trace concentrations are catalog-derived not paper-stated (hence medium confidence). Wolfe's vitamins (MD-VS) were also purchased but were NOT added to the aerobic growth medium (only Wolfe's minerals + B1 were), so the vitamin mix is deliberately excluded. Exact M9 salt amounts are the standard formulation, not spelled out in the paper.
